title: leptinella reptans
intro: Leptinella reptans, also known as creeping kaikorua or creeping wirewort, is a small, perennial plant native to New Zealand and parts of Australia. Leptinella reptans has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and skin infections. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antiseptic activities. In herbal preparations, Leptinella reptans is often used as a tincture or infusion, and it is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
